it:19.2: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
it:19.3: "One thing, when arising in the world, arises for the welfare of many, for the happiness of many, for the welfare and happiness of many beings, both human and divine. Which one thing? Concord in the Saṅgha. When the Saṅgha is in concord, there are no arguments with one another, no abuse of one another, no ganging up on one another, no abandoning of one another. Then those with little confidence [in the teaching] become confident, while those already confident become even more so."
it:19.4: Blissful is concord in the Saṅgha. One who assists in concord — delighting in concord, judicious — isn't barred from safety from bondage. Having brought concord to the Saṅgha, he rejoices for an aeon in heaven.
it:19.5: This, too, was the meaning of what was said by the Blessed One, so I have heard.
